element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Single-frame timing required for optimal damage; whip can cancel landing lag if timed correctly
2x slower than walking; teaches spatial navigation on vertical axes
Spawn height tied to Simon's Y position; player can control spawn location through positioning
Drop hearts and subweapons; teaches resource management
Each consumes hearts; stopwatch freezes enemies; requires tactical selection
Two jump types (short, high); manipulable via 7-frame detection window; cannot be outwalked on short jumps
Movement does not affect Simon's walking speed; erratic timing; waiting required in Stage 10
Only Stages 4 and 6; detect from fixed Y-corridor; can be delayed or provoked with timed jumps
Spawn direction and height tied to Simon's position and orientation
Double-jump exploit: timed damage mid-air gains +14 Y coordinates; used to bypass platform-gap sections
Escalating complexity; final boss (Dracula, Stage 18) requires pattern recognition
Share boss HP meter but have independent HP addresses; require sustained whip assault

typical_relief_triggers: Climbing stairs (slowed but predictable enemy behavior)
Stage transitions (brief respite before new enemy patterns)
typical_tension_triggers: Screen edge enemy spawns (bats, medusa heads)
Jumping across moving platforms with erratic timing
Knockback landing from high jumps (loss of control)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Waves of escalating pressure punctuated by candle breaks; early stages introduce mechanics gently with safe-height enemies; mid-stages (4-8) layer moving platforms with airborne pursuits; final third introduces boss battles that consolidate all prior mechanics into pattern-based endurance tests

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Candles positioned off main path or at platform edges signal optional exploration; whip-reach distance reveals hittable objects
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Certain platforms are reachable only via knockback momentum exploit (Stage 4, Stage 7, pre-boss sections); hanging bats can be jumped over by moving to different Y-corridor; hidden shortcut paths exist via damage-boost timing

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stages 1-3 teach whip timing, enemy spawn mechanics, and basic platforming with gentle enemy introduction (bats at medium height, slow hunchbacks); ample candle resources
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 4-9 compound mechanics: moving platforms gain erratic timing, airborne enemies spawn more frequently and at variable heights, hunchback packs demand momentum-boost exploitation, subweapon economy tightens; first major skill bottleneck at Stage 4 climbing section
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stages 10-18 abandon gentle pacing; boss encounters (Frankenstein/Mummies/Death/Dracula) demand pattern mastery; moving platform sections become longer and more demanding; enemy density increases; final confrontation consolidates all mechanics
notable_spikes: Stage 4 staircase with hanging bats (introduces momentum-boost requirement)
Stage 6 (first boss: Frankenstein's Monster; requires sustained focus)
Stage 12 (Mummy twins; dual-boss mechanic, shared HP pool)
Stage 18 (Dracula final form; multi-phase pattern with limited recovery time)

notable_level_patterns.description: Candle placement mirrors tension cycles: clustered in open areas during low-pressure sections, sparse during enemy-heavy sequences. Destruction yields hearts (health recovery) or subweapons (tactical tools). This creates resource-driven risk/reward decision points where players choose between healing and weaponizing.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 2 (multiple candle clusters enable subweapon experimentation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Staircases appear in predictable locations to gate upward progression. Simon's stair-climbing is 2x slower than walking, creating temporal vulnerability windows. Enemies often spawn near stair tops, forcing players to climb defensively or use knockback skips.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 4 (staircase followed by hanging bat, must clip ceiling with momentum boost to proceed efficiently)

notable_level_patterns.description: Early stages (1-3) feature bats spawned singly or in pairs at predictable heights. Mid-stages (5-8) introduce simultaneous spawns of multiple bat and medusa head types. Late stages (10-18) pack screens with overlapping spawn waves, forcing navigation rather than combat.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 7 (sustained bat and medusa spawn concurrent with moving platforms)

notable_level_patterns.description: Platforms move independently of Simon's movement speed, creating precision timing requirements. Early appearances (Stage 4) are brief and slow-moving; later stages (Stages 5, 10) feature longer sequences with erratic speed variation, demanding frame-perfect jumps or knockback manipulation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 10 (extended moving platform section with mandatory waiting, Stage 10 noted for 'quite erratic' movement speed)

notable_level_patterns.description: Bosses introduce after Stage 6 with increasing behavioral complexity. Each boss (Frankenstein, Mummy twins, Death, Dracula) has distinct attack patterns (high jump, low jump, scythe arc, fireball spread) requiring player adaptation rather than reflexive whipping. Multi-phase encounters extend endurance requirements.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 18 (Dracula multi-phase; final gate consolidates all prior learning)
